questions.question: Which statement correctly describes Compton scattering?
options: The photon is completely absorbed by an inner-shell electron and disappears
The photon transfers part of its energy to a loosely bound electron and continues in a new direction
The photon converts into an electron-positron pair near a nucleus
The photon bounces off the nucleus with no energy loss
questions.explanation: Compton scattering is a partial energy transfer: the photon survives, deflected with reduced energy. Those scattered photons are the fog that degrades radiographic contrast.

questions.question: A Co-60 source held 20 curies about 10.5 years ago (two half-lives of 5.27 years). About what does it hold today?
questions.explanation: Two half-lives means two halvings: 20 to 10 to 5 curies. The halving-table method works for any isotope once you know its half-life.

questions.question: Using an approximate HVL of 0.5 in. of steel for Ir-192, about how much steel reduces a beam from 800 mR/h to 100 mR/h?
questions.explanation: 800 to 100 is a factor of 8, which is three halvings - three HVLs, or about 1.5 in. of steel for Ir-192 (narrow-beam approximation).

questions.question: A survey meter reads 144 mR/h at 4 feet from a source. At about what distance does the reading fall to 9 mR/h?
questions.explanation: 144/9 = 16, an intensity factor of 16, which by the inverse square law is a distance factor of 4: 4 ft × 4 = 16 ft.

questions.question: A technique calls for 4 minutes with 100 curies at an 18-inch SFD. Today the source reads 50 curies and the SFD must be 27 inches. What is the corrected time?
questions.explanation: Decay factor: 100/50 = 2. Distance factor: (27/18)² = 2.25. New time: 4 × 2 × 2.25 = 18 minutes. Corrections multiply, one factor at a time.

questions.question: A code caps geometric unsharpness at 0.020 in. With an effective source size of 0.25 in. and a source-side-of-part-to-film distance of 2 in., the minimum source-to-object distance is:
questions.explanation: Rearrange Ug = F·t/d to d = F × t / Ug = 0.25 × 2 / 0.020 = 25 in. Geometry is checked before exposure time is ever computed.

questions.question: An X-ray exposure chart can be used directly, without corrections, only when:
options: The kilovoltage is below the chart's maximum
The film system, target density, screens, SFD, and material all match the chart's stated conditions
The part is steel of any thickness
The machine is from the same manufacturer
questions.explanation: A chart is a promise with conditions - it is valid only inside them. Any change of distance, film, density, screens, or material must be corrected by the appropriate ratio or factor.

questions.question: Radiographic sensitivity - the ability to demonstrate small detail - improves when:
options: Contrast improves, even if edges blur badly
Definition improves, even if the image goes flat
Both contrast and definition improve on a film within its density window
Density alone is pushed as high as the illuminator allows
questions.explanation: Sensitivity is the outcome of contrast (signal size) and definition (edge sharpness) standing on adequate density - starving any one of the three degrades what the IQI demonstrates.

questions.question: Why do experienced film interpreters aim for the upper half of the allowable density window?
options: Film gradient - and therefore film contrast - increases with density up the straight-line region
Higher density always means shorter exposures
Codes award extra sensitivity credit above density 3.0
questions.explanation: Up the straight-line region of the characteristic curve the gradient steepens, so the same thickness change produces a bigger density difference - more contrast, as long as the illuminator can still read through it.

questions.question: When an IQI cannot be placed on the source side and is placed on the film side instead, what does typical code practice require?
options: Nothing - the placement makes no difference
A lead letter F adjacent to the IQI so the placement is evident on the film
Removing the shim so the plaque reads thinner
questions.explanation: Film-side placement is the easier geometry, so it must be flagged: a lead letter F appears beside the IQI image to tell every reader how the sensitivity was demonstrated.
